column2: The patient's discharge instructions provide comprehensive details about their condition, treatment, and recommended follow-ups.
column3: Discharge Instructions: Dear Ms. , You were admitted to because you had a fall. It is unclear why you fell. We believe that you suffered a mild concussion since you have no memory before or after the fall. You had head imaging which showed that your ventricles were enlarged. Therefore, you were evaluated by Neurosurgery to check your VP shunt. They found that your VP shunt was working properly. You also had imaging of your shunt which showed that it is working well and that the shunt settings are appropriate; the imaging was unchanged from the last time you had your shunt evaluated. Your pacemaker was also interrogated by Cardiology, and they found that pacemaker was working well. You were also evaluated by physical therapy who felt that you would benefit from discharge to a rehabilitation facility to help you with your walking and build your strength. Please follow-up with your outpatient providers as instructed below. Thank you for allowing us to participate in your care. All best wishes for your recovery.
